title: auto recipe
description: Documentation schema for an auto recipe (the named, file-backed JSON declaration of a workflow topology). The hand-rolled validator lib/recipes.py::validate() is the ENFORCER — it is pure-stdlib (no jsonschema dependency, install-anywhere) and applies cross-field integrity rules this schema only sketches (depends_on integrity, the iteration<->emit_templates pairing rule, phase membership). See docs/contracts/recipe-format.md for the LOCKED prose contract. If this schema and validate() ever disagree, validate() wins.

name.description: Recipe id; non-empty, matches the filename stem.

description.description: One-line summary; shown in the picker + rendered card.

default_backend.description: Backend used when --backend is not passed.

phase_order.description: The run's phase sequence. Validated STRUCTURALLY (v0.6.0/U6): every element a non-empty string; terminal_phase, every unit/emit_template phase, and every phase_transition from/to MUST be a member. Default ["plan","handoff","work"].

terminal_phase.description: Phase whose completion ends the run. Default "work". MUST be a member of phase_order.

producer.description: MUST be a V1-registered phase-boundary producer name (validate() rejects any other). iterate_template is within-phase and never named here.

steps.description: The declared units. MUST be non-empty for a work-only (["work"]) recipe.

iteration.description: (v0.3.0, additive) Outcomes-gated iteration block — see recipe-format.md §6.

emit_templates.description: (v0.3.0, additive) Map of <template_name> -> partial unit shape materialized by iterate_template — see recipe-format.md §7.

expected_emit_outputs.description: (v0.3.0, additive — F4) Unit ids materialized by a phase-boundary producer, declared so depends_on integrity accepts them — see recipe-format.md §8.

plan_presatisfied.description: (v0.4.3, KTD-15) Declares the plan phase already-satisfied (W). Coherence checked by validate().

python_hook.description: RESERVED — parses but the V1 engine ignores it. The ONLY tolerated unknown-ish top-level key.

phase.description: MUST be a member of phase_order.

depends_on.description: Unit ids this unit waits for. Each member must be in steps[], iterate-shape ({id_prefix}{positive_int}), or in expected_emit_outputs — see recipe-format.md §3/§8.

invokes.description: What the unit invokes — backend_op plus optional recipe-side metadata. prompt_template (if present) MUST be a relative path with no '..' segments and no leading '/'.

verification.description: (v0.7.0, U2 — KTD-1) Optional array of typed, checkable done-conditions layered onto the existing iterate/advance/exit gate decision. Capped at 16 to bound gate-evaluation cost. Criterion ids must be unique within the unit. Shape per skills/auto-design/references/verification-taxonomy.md.

verification_criterion.description: A single typed verification criterion. `type` selects the allowed key set (NOT a flat union): a programmatic criterion carrying `prompt`, or a human criterion carrying `argv`, is rejected as an unknown field for its type.

oneOf.title: programmatic
oneOf.description: A deterministic check the engine runs with no model in the loop.

timeout_sec.description: Optional positive int; default 30. Booleans rejected.

oneOf.title: model_judge / advisor_judge
oneOf.description: model_judge = the dispatched work agent's own verdict; advisor_judge = a stronger transcript-aware second opinion from the `advisor` tool (driver-evaluated).

oneOf.title: human
oneOf.description: A checkpoint only a human can clear — routes through auto's pause handoff.

prompt.description: Optional non-empty prompt shown at the checkpoint.

programmatic_check.description: The programmatic `check` discriminator: the string "exit_zero", or a single-key object {stdout_contains: str} | {stdout_equals: str}.
